Notice Of And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages In First Amended Complaint As To Avina Estate; And Points And Authorities And Request For Judicial Notice In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS AMERICA AVINA, ESTATE DANIEL and MEDARDO AVINA's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages In First Amended Complaint As To Avina Estate; And Points And Authorities And Request For Judicial Notice In Support Thereof is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported (D501)

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Wolford, Esq.(415-265-2897) for Plaintiff Jose Martin Canales.
Scott Freedman, Esq. of Zacks & Freedman (415-956-8100) for Defendants Working Dirt Management, Gingher & Farag.
Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR#: 5229, Telephone Number (415-828-1919).

Hearing is held. Argued. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Temporary Restraining Order is dissolved without prejudice and Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction is Denied without Prejudice (as fully reported in the reporter's notes).

The Court orders:
1. The Landlord to meet and confer with Plaintiff's counsel to arrange an appropriate, safe, mutually agreeable date and time to allow tenants access to the property for a date and time for tenants to have access to the property.
2. The Court Denies the request on the issue re: changing the locks and chaining the gate.
3. The Court orders the parties to wait for the DBI decision on the relocation money to be final.
4. The Court at this time denies without prejudice the Contract between tenant and landlord for relocation money to the Plaintiff's ability to bring a motion for breach of contract.
5. The court orders the Temporary restraining order dissolved withour prejudice. The tenants are to come back to Court in the event that the landlord fails to comply with the law which requires him to pay reasonable relocation expenses under the health and safety code 1797.5.
6. The Court orders Mr. Freedman to prepare the proposed order approved as to form. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto. CSR#: 5229, Telephone Number (415-828-1919), Reported, D501.
